name: Hasenpfeffer (Spicy Braised Rabbit)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Frozen rabbit, thawed and cut
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle rabbit with salt. Coat with 1/3 cup flour; shake off excess.
2. Fry bacon in a Dutch oven over medium heat until crisp; remove bacon and drain on paper towels.
3. Brown a few pieces of rabbit in hot bacon fat; remove browned pieces. Repeat with remaining rabbit. Remove all but 2 tablespoons fat.
4. Cook and stir shallots and garlic in hot fat in the Dutch oven until shallots are tender, about 4 minutes.
5. Stir in wine, 1 cup water, and the instant bouillon. Heat to boiling.
6. Stir in jelly, peppercorns, bay leaf, rosemary, and thyme. Return rabbit and bacon to the Dutch oven.
7. Reduce heat. Cover and simmer until rabbit is tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
8. Remove bay leaf and discard. Place rabbit on a warm platter; keep warm while preparing gravy.
9. Stir lemon juice into liquid in the Dutch oven.
10. Mix 3 tablespoons water and 2 tablespoons flour in a covered jar.
11. Mix 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme leaves in a cheesecloth bag.
12. Stir for 1 minute. If gravy is too thick, stir in more water until desired consistency.
13. Serve gravy with rabbit.
